age.description: The age negative values will give you a baby.

set-maturity.description: If set to false won't change the state of the animal.

apply-age-lock.description: If set to true locks the animal at the set age.

max-targets.description: Max targets for the AOE. 0 means no max.

circle-shape.description: Whether the horizontal radius of the AOE should be circular.

cone.description: If point-blank is set to true, which it is by default, this will make the AOE a cone instead of it being 360 degrees around the caster. The best way to explain it is that it works like the sweep attack on swords. Value is specified in degrees.

horizontal-cone.description: . Same as above, but this checks the horizontal cone instead.

vertical-radius.description: Vertical hit radius for the AOE.

horizontal-radius.description: Horizontal hit radius for the AOE.

min-horizontal-radius.description: . Minimal horizontal hit radius for the AOE.

min-vertical-radius.description: . Minimal vertical hit radius for the AOE.

use-proximity.description: Defines if the AOE should select targets by proximity. This is only useful if you have defined max-targets.

ignore-radius.description: . When true, it makes the AOE target all entities in all worlds instead of having a radius constraint. Use other options to limit the target list, including target-modifiers like usual.

reverse-proximity.description: Defines reverse proximity, if use-proximity is enabled. This makes the AOE look for further away targets instead.

point-blank.description: While set to true, when cast the start location of the AOE will be the caster's location. When it's set to false, the AOE center will be the block the caster pointed at while casting the spell.

fail-if-no-targets.description: If set to false, if the AOE fails to find targets, it won't play any effects specified on it.

pass-targeting.description: When false, sub-spells will recheck their individual can-target lists.

spell-source-in-center.description: If point-blank is set to false and this to true, it will attempt to make the source of all targeted spells the center of the AOE. For example, if the AOE casts a HomingMissileSpell, the missile will spawn in the center of the AOE.

tolerance.description: Used when determining what falls into the scanned area when shape takes the value of x_cylinder, y_cylinder, z_cylinder, or ellipsoid. A value lesser than one is more strict (and thus, fewer blocks are scanned), while a value greater than one is more lax (and thus, more blocks are scanned).

inner-tolerance.description: Same as above, but used in the inner radius.

absolute-offset.description: Applies an offset to the area being scanned.

relative-offset.description: Applies an offset to the area being scanned.

shape.description: Shape of the scan. Supported options are box, x_cylinder, y_cylinder, z_cylinder, and ellipsoid.

x-variable.description: If a block is successfully found, its coordinates are saved to the variables specified in these options. This occurs before location-modifiers is checked for the block.

y-variable.description: If a block is successfully found, its coordinates are saved to the variables specified in these options. This occurs before location-modifiers is checked for the block.

z-variable.description: If a block is successfully found, its coordinates are saved to the variables specified in these options. This occurs before location-modifiers is checked for the block.

spell.description: Defines a sub-spell to cast when a block is successfully found.  if the sub-spell supports targeting a location, the block found is supplied as the target.

point-blank.description: When true, the caster's location is targeted instead.

block-coords.description: The blocks scanned when casting the spell are reached by applying an offset to the origin location of the spell. When block-coords: true, the origin's coordinates are modified to be block coordinates, eliminating the fractional component of the coordinates.

blocks.description: When defined, any blocks scanned must match at least one of the block data specified.

denied-blocks.description: When defined, any blocks scanned must not match any of the block data specified.

scan-modifiers.description: Location modifiers (https://github.com/TheComputerGeek2/MagicSpells/wiki/modifiers) which is checked against each block in the scan. location-modifiers are only checked for the initial block.

duration.description: Buff spell duration specified in seconds before the buff expires. Set to 0 to define an infinite duration.

toggle.description: Defines whether the spell can be deactivated while still active. It will only apply cooldown when its duration fades.

targeted.description: Defines if this spell should become a targeted spell.

cancel-on-join.description: Defines whether the buff spell will cancel if the caster joins the game.

cancel-on-move.description: Defines whether the buff spell will cancel if the caster moves.

cancel-on-death.description: Defines whether the buff spell will cancel if the caster dies.

cancel-on-logout.description: Defines whether the buff spell will cancel if the caster leaves the game.

cancel-on-teleport.description: Defines whether the buff spell will cancel if the server emits a teleport event for the caster.

cancel-on-spell-cast.description: Defines whether the buff spell will cancel if the caster casts a spell.  this cancels all spells if the Spell Filter is left undefined.

cancel-on-take-damage.description: Defines whether the buff spell will cancel if the caster receives damage.

cancel-on-give-damage.description: Defines whether the buff spell will cancel if the caster deals damage to another entity.

cancel-on-change-world.description: Defines whether the buff spell will cancel if the caster switches worlds.

cancel-affects-target.description: If the buff spell is targeted, cancelling logic will be affected by and to the target instead.

real-time-duration.description: . When true, duration is tracked using real time, instead of ticks. As such, lag will not impact the duration of the buff spell.

power-affects-duration.description: Defines whether Spell Power can affect the spell's duration.

end-spell-from-target.description: Defines caster of the spell-on-end. If the spell isn't targeted, the "target" is technically the caster, so they cast the spell.

str-fade.description: Message sent after the buff ends.

spell-on-end.description: Sub-spell that will be cast whenever the buff spell reaches the end of its duration.

permanent.description: Whether the armor is permanent, or temporary. If permanent, it will be removable, and if temporary, it will not be able to be taken off for the duration.

replace.description: Whether the conjured armor should replace any armor the caster currently has on. If this is not enabled, and there is ANY armor currently equipped, the spell will not be able to be cast.

helmet.description: The helmet to conjure to the player. Supports Magic Item String (https://github.com/TheComputerGeek2/MagicSpells/wiki/Magic-Item-String) and Magic Item Config (https://github.com/TheComputerGeek2/MagicSpells/wiki/Magic-Item-Config) format.

chestplate.description: The chestplate to conjure to the player.

leggings.description: The leggings to conjure to the player.

boots.description: The boots to conjure to the player.

str-has-armor.default: "You cannot cast this spell if you are wearing armor."
str-has-armor.description: String message to send if the caster already has armor on and replace is not set to true.

can-cast-with-item.description: Defines if this spell can be cast with items.  with this false, spells can still be bound as cast items and cycled.

can-cast-by-command.description: Defines if this spell can be cast with the /cast (spell) command.

relative-offset.description: Modifies the start location of the beam by the defined vector.

target-relative-offset.description: Modifies the entity target location of the beam by the defined vector.

hit-radius.description: Radius around the beam that will be collided with.

max-distance.description: Max distance it will travel before removal.

vertical-rotation.description: . Rotates the beam on the vertical axis constantly.

horizontal-rotation.description: . Rotates the beam on the horizontal axis constantly.

y-offset.description: Starting position of the beam according to the caster's location. (Overrides relative-offset)

gravity.description: How fast the beam falls, roughly in blocks per travelled distance.

interval.description: Defines how many blocks the Beam should travel per step.

rotation.description: Rotation of the beam over time and distance to the degree given.

beam-spread.description: Effectively the opposite of accuracy. A higher number means less accuracy.

beam-vertical-spread.description: Effectively the opposite of vertical accuracy. A higher number means less accuracy. (overrides beam-spread)

beam-horizontal-spread.description: Effectively the opposite of horizontal accuracy. A higher number means less accuracy. (overrides beam-spread)

change-pitch.description: Whether to change the start location's pitch.

stop-on-hit-entity.description: Should the beam stop itself when it hits an entity?

stop-on-hit-ground.description: Should the beam stop itself when it hits the ground?

spell.description: Sub-spell cast on hit.

spell-on-end.description: Sub-spell cast on beam's end position.

spell-on-travel.description: Sub-spell cast on each block along the beam's length

spell-on-hit-ground.description: Sub-spell cast when the beam hits ground.

spell-on-entity-location.description: Sub-spell to cast when the beam hits a target, firing at the location where the beam hits the entity. For example, if the beam hits the head of a zombie, the spell provided will be cast at the zombie's head. It must be a targeted spell that can hit the ground.

bindable-items.description: Items this spell may be bound to.

allowed-spells.description: Spells allowed to be bound by this Bind spell.

check-can-cast-with-item.description: . When false, the can-cast-with-item option will no longer prevent binding.

str-no-spell.default: "You do not know a spell by that name."

str-cant-bind-item.default: "That spell cannot be bound to that item."

str-spell-cant-bind.default: "That spell cannot be bound like this."

str-cant-blink.description: If the blink fails it will send this message.

bow-name.description: When defined, only bows with names that match this value exactly can trigger the bow spell.

disallowed-bow-names.description: When defined, any bows with names contained in this list cannot trigger the bow spell.

bow-items.description: When defined, only bows that match one of the listed items (using the Magic Item String (https://github.com/TheComputerGeek2/MagicSpells/wiki/Magic-Item-String) format) can trigger the spell.

ammo-items.description: When defined, only ammo (items used to fire a projectile) that match one of the listed magic items can trigger the spell.

disallowed-bow-items.description: When defined, only bows that do not match one of the list items (using the Magic Item String (https://github.com/TheComputerGeek2/MagicSpells/wiki/Magic-Item-String) format) can trigger the spell. This option takes precedence over the option bow-items.

disallowed-ammo-items.description: When defined, only ammo (items used to fire a projectile) that do not match one of the list magic items can trigger the spell. This option takes precedence over the option ammo-items.

spell-on-hit-entity.description: Sub-spell cast when the arrow associated with the bow spell hits an entity.

spell-on-hit-ground.description: Sub-spell cast when the arrow associated with the bow spell hits a block.

spell-on-hit-entity-location.description: Sub-spell cast when the arrow associated with the bow spell hits an entity, firing at the location the arrow hits the entity. For example, if the arrow hits the head of a zombie, the spell provided will be cast at the zombie's head. Must be a targeted spell that can hit the ground.

bindable.description: Defines whether the bow spell can be bound to an item.

require-bind.description: Requires bow spell to be bound to be cast.

cancel-shot.description: Cancels the bow's arrow shot if the bow spell is successfully cast.

deny-offhand.description: If true, the bow spell will not be cast if the arrow is shot from a bow in the offhand.

remove-arrow.description: Removes the arrow associated with a bow spell after casting spells from hitting the ground or an entity.

use-bow-force.description: Uses the bow's force when shot as the power for the spells being cast. 0 force translates to 0 power; 1 force translates to 1 power. Spells by default have 1 power.

cancel-shot-on-fail.description: When true, if the bow spell fails to cast, the bow's arrow shot is cancelled.

minimum-force.description: Minimum force (how far the bow is pulled back) for the bow spell to trigger, in the interval [0, 1].

maximum-force.description: Maximum force (how far the bow is pulled back) for the bow spell to trigger, in the interval [0, 1].

preferred-slot.description: Forces a conjured item to appear in this slot, if add-to-inventory is set to true, and no other items are in the slot.

random-velocity.description: If add-to-inventory is false and this is greater than 0, the item(s) will spawn with this velocity in a random direction.

offhand.description: Whether or not the item should appear in the offhand slot if add-to-inventory is set to true.

ignore-max-stack-size.description: Whether the spell should ignore max stack size for the conjured item.

power-affects-quantity.description: Whether the spell power affects the number of items dropped.

force-update-inventory.description: Whether the spell should force an inventory update.

damage-type.description: Specifies which damage type to deal damage with.

credit-caster.description: Specifies if the damage should be credited to the caster.

spell-damage-type.description: Optional tag used in other spells.

data-element.description: Data elements can be found here DataLivingEntity Or DataEntity.

variable-name.description: Which variable to place the data-element in.

duration.description: Duration specified in ticks before the glow expires. Set to 0 to define an infinite duration.

color.description: Determines the color of the glow (ignore Deprecated flags).

falling-block-damage.description: The amount of damage caused when a falling block lands on a player.

falling-block-max-height.description: . The maximum amount of damage caused.

velocity-type.description: Options: up, random, random_up, down, toward, away, none

prevent-landing-blocks.description: If this is true, blocks will disappear rather than land on the ground.

block-types-to-throw.description: A list of block materials to throw. If this is not defined, it will throw all solid blocks except bedrock.

block-types-to-remove.description: A list of block materials to remove completely. If this is not defined, it will remove all non-solid blocks.

disarmable-items.description: List every item with the Magic Item String (https://github.com/TheComputerGeek2/MagicSpells/wiki/Magic-Item-String) format you want to be disarmable via this spell. Leave blank to disarm all items.

disarm-duration.description: How long the disarm lasts in ticks. If dont-drop is false, will prevent the item from being picked back up by the target for the duration.

dont-drop.description: If true, the item will simply vanish, then reappear in the chosen slot. If false, the item will drop on the ground and have to be picked up again by the target.

prevent-theft.description: If true, this will prevent anyone but the targeted player from picking up the dropped item.

str-invalid-item.description: String message, sent to the caster if the target's held item cannot be disarmed.

discover.description: When true, the player will discover the recipe. When false, they will un-discover it.

cancel-on-change-world.description: Defines whether the buff spell will cancel if the caster switches worlds.

force.description: . When enabled, attribute types not normally present on an entity are forcefully registered, allowing normally incompatible modifiers to be applied. Any attributes added this way will use the attribute's default base value.

remove.description: . Removes attributes instead of applying them when enabled.

toggle.description: Toggles the applied modification.

permanent.description: . When disabled, attributes added by the spell no longer save with the entity. These attributes persist when the plugin unloads.

block-type.description: Block data used for the tomb.

duration.description: Duration of the entomb (in ticks).

allow-breaking.description: If the target is allowed to break the tomb.

close-top-and-bottom.description: Determines if the top and bottom of the tomb are open or not.

block-destroy-message.description: Message to send when someone tries to break the entomb.

add-fire.description: Whether fire should be added into the explosion.

simulate-tnt.description: Whether the explosion should simulate tnt.

ignore-cancelled.description: Whether a cancelled explosion should be ignored.

prevent-block-damage.description: Whether the explosion should prevent blocks from taking damage.

prevent-player-damage.description: Whether the explosion should prevent players from taking damage.

prevent-animal-damage.description: Whether the explosion should prevent animals from taking damage.

command-to-block.description: Leave out the first /. The command is blocked if it starts with a listed item.

command-to-execute.description: Leave out the first /. You may list DELAY  to delay listed commands.

command-to-execute-later.description: Works just like command-to-execute after command-delay. The only placeholders this option supports before 4.0  are %a for the caster's Entity Name, and %t for the target's if they exist.

temporary-permissions.description: A list of temporary permission nodes to grant for casting specific commands they won't have permission to execute. Using temporary-op is easier.

command-delay.description: The delay in server ticks to execute the commands in command-to-execute-later.

temporary-op.description: It's almost instant, so you don't have to worry about players using commands they are not supposed to while they are opped.

block-chat-output.description: This blocks message chat output from commands, when enabled. There are two variants of this handler: one with the ProtocolLib plugin installed and one without. The one without is unreliable, as it only works for certain commands, and below 4.0  will send str-blocked-output instead, even if it's empty (a blank chat line). The one with ProtocolLib was broken since 1.19 for versions below 4.0 .

store-chat-output.description: . String variable name to store the command output in. Requires ProtocolLib.

do-variable-replacement.description: Lets you use string expressions in commands. Before 4.0  %a & %t worked regardless of this option.

execute-as-target-instead.description: Executes specified commands as the targeted player if require-player-target is set to true.

use-target-variables-instead.description: If do-variable-replacement and require-player-target are set to true, it will parse the targeted player's variable values for the %var parameter.

str-blocked-output.description: block-chat-output describes when this message will be sent. Due to a limitation, it has to be sent even if it's an empty line.

str-cant-use-command.default: "&4You don't have permission to do that."
str-cant-use-command.description: The message sent when the player attempts to execute a command from command-to-block.

crop-type.description: The crop type to grow if the farmland is empty.

spell-on-entity-location.description: Sub-spell to cast when the missile hits a target. Must be a targeted spell that can hit the ground.

spell-after-duration.description: Sub-spell to cast when the missile reaches its max-duration. Must be a targeted spell that can hit the ground, depending on how the options above are set up. (requires hit-air-after-duration)

max-duration.description: The maximum duration this missile is allowed to exist. (in seconds)

y-offset.description: Modifies the start y offset. (overrides relative-offset)

hit-radius.description: How far from the center of the missile to look for entities to hit.

vertical-hit-radius.description: How far vertically from the center of the missile to look for entities to hit.

projectile-velocity.description: How fast the missile moves, roughly in blocks per second.

tick-interval.description: Defines (in server ticks) how often the missile ticks to check modifiers, check hitboxes, play effects, etc.

spell-interval.description: Used with the hit-air-during option, casts the spell-on-hit-air each defined interval. This interval is every tick-interval * spell-interval ticks.

special-effect-interval.description: How often to play Spell Effects on the special position. This interval is every tick-interval * special-effect-interval ticks.

intermediate-special-effect-locations.description: How often to play the particle effects between travelled locations (in ticks)

projectile-horiz-offset.description: patch 1. An offset of the direction the projectile initially moves in degrees

projectile-vert-offset.description: patch 1. An offset of the direction the projectile initially moves vertically in tangent values.

projectile-vert-spread.description: patch 1. Effectively the opposite of vertical accuracy. A higher number means less accuracy.

location.description: The location at which the spell will be cast, written as worldname,x,y,z,yaw,pitch. yaw and pitch are optional.

spell.description: The sub-spell to cast at the configured location.

delay.description: Defines how long, in ticks, before the loop starts. : With negative values, the iterations are done immediately, instead of at the beginning of the next tick.

duration.description: Defines how long, in ticks, the loop will last. If less than or equal to 0, the loop will last indefinitely unless iterations are specified.

interval.description: Defines how long, in ticks, the interval between casting the spells. : When <=0, all of the iterations of the loop are done at once.

iterations.description: Defines how many total iterations the loop will do. If less than or equal to 0, the loop will last indefinitely unless duration is specified.

y-offset.description: Vertical offset when casting at a location

targeted.description: If true, depending on require-entity-target and point-blank, the loop will target an entity or location. If false, spells will be cast without an entity or location target.

point-blank.description: If true and require-entity-target: false, the loop will target the initial location of the caster.

stop-on-fail.description: If true, if a spell fails to cast, the loop will stop.

only-count-on-success.description: If true, an iteration of the loop only counts towards iterations if all of the subspells in spells succeed.

pass-targeting.description: When false, sub-spells will recheck their individual can-target lists.

cancel-on-death.description: If true, dying will cancel the loop. Will always cancel if targeting a non-player entity.

require-entity-target.description: If true, targets an entity for the loop.

cast-random-spell-instead.description: If true, the loop will instead randomly cast one spell in spells each iteration.

skip-first.description: If true, skips loop-modifiers, variable-mods-loop, loop-target-modifiers, loop-location-modifiers and variable-mods-target-loop on the first iteration of the loop.

skip-first-loop-modifiers.description: If true, skip loop-modifiers on the first iteration of the loop. Uses the value of skip-first as its default.

skip-first-variable-mods-loop.description: If true, skip variable-mods-loop on the first iteration of the loop. Uses the value of skip-first as its default.

skip-first-loop-target-modifiers.description: If true, skip loop-target-modifiers on the first iteration of the loop. Uses the value of skip-first as its default.

skip-first-loop-location-modifiers.description: If true, skip loop-location-modifiers on the first iteration of the loop. Uses the value of skip-first as its default.

skip-first-variable-mods-target-loop.description: If true, skip variable-mods-target-loop on the first iteration of the loop. Uses the value of skip-first as its default.

str-fade-self.description: Message sent to the caster when the loop ends.

str-fade-target.description: Message sent to the target when the loop ends.

spell-on-end.description: Sub-spell casted when the loop ends.

spells.description: List of sub-spells casted on each iteration of the loop.

variable-mods-loop.description: List of variable modifications performed on the caster on each iteration of the loop. Performed before loop-modifiers and loop-target-modifiers are checked.

variable-mods-target-loop.description: List of variable modifications performed on the target on each iteration of the loop. Performed before loop-modifiers and loop-target-modifiers are checked.

loop-modifiers.description: List of modifiers (https://github.com/TheComputerGeek2/MagicSpells/wiki/modifiers) checked at the beginning of each iteration of the loop. Checked after variable-mods-loop and variable-mods-target-loop are performed.

loop-target-modifiers.description: List of target modifiers (https://github.com/TheComputerGeek2/MagicSpells/wiki/modifiers) checked at the beginning of each iteration of the loop. Checked after variable-mods-loop and variable-mods-target-loop are performed.

loop-location-modifiers.description: List of location modifiers (https://github.com/TheComputerGeek2/MagicSpells/wiki/modifiers) checked at the beginning of each iteration of the loop. Checked after variable-mods-loop and variable-mods-target-loop are performed.

duration.description: Duration of the spell (in ticks).

str-duration.description: Message sent to the caster and targeted player when the bond ends.

can-cast-with-item.description: Defines if this spell can be cast with items.  with this false, spells can still be bound as cast items and cycled.

can-cast-by-command.description: Defines if this spell can be cast with the /cast (spell) command.

velocity.description: The velocity the items are being pulled at.

teleport-items.description: Whether to teleport the items to you or not (ignores velocity).

force-pickup.description: Whether to force the pickup of the items or not.

remove-item-gravity.description: Whether to remove gravity from items or not.

remove-item-friction.description: . Removes friction experienced with the air and ground.

duration.description: Buff spell duration specified in seconds before the buff expires. Set to 0 to define an infinite duration.

toggle.description: Defines whether the spell can be deactivated while still active. It will only apply cooldown when its duration fades.

targeted.description: Defines if this spell should become a targeted spell.

cancel-on-join.description: Defines whether the buff spell will cancel if the caster joins the game.

cancel-on-move.description: Defines whether the buff spell will cancel if the caster moves.

cancel-on-death.description: Defines whether the buff spell will cancel if the caster dies.

cancel-on-logout.description: Defines whether the buff spell will cancel if the caster leaves the game.

cancel-on-teleport.description: Defines whether the buff spell will cancel if the server emits a teleport event for the caster.

cancel-on-spell-cast.description: Defines whether the buff spell will cancel if the caster casts a spell.  this cancels all spells if the Spell Filter is left undefined.

cancel-on-take-damage.description: Defines whether the buff spell will cancel if the caster receives damage.

cancel-on-give-damage.description: Defines whether the buff spell will cancel if the caster deals damage to another entity.

cancel-on-change-world.description: Defines whether the buff spell will cancel if the caster switches worlds.

cancel-affects-target.description: If the buff spell is targeted, cancelling logic will be affected by and to the target instead.

real-time-duration.description: . When true, duration is tracked using real time, instead of ticks. As such, lag will not impact the duration of the buff spell.

power-affects-duration.description: Defines whether Spell Power can affect the spell's duration.

end-spell-from-target.description: Defines caster of the spell-on-end. If the spell isn't targeted, the "target" is technically the caster, so they cast the spell.

str-fade.description: Message sent after the buff ends.

spell-on-end.description: Sub-spell that will be cast whenever the buff spell reaches the end of its duration.

regen-mod-amt.description: The amount of additional mana to receive each mana regen "tick".

can-cast-with-item.description: Defines if this spell can be cast with items.  with this false, spells can still be bound as cast items and cycled.

can-cast-by-command.description: Defines if this spell can be cast with the /cast (spell) command.

mana.description: How much mana to restore. Negative value takes mana instead.

can-cast-with-item.description: Defines if this spell can be cast with items.  with this false, spells can still be bound as cast items and cycled.

can-cast-by-command.description: Defines if this spell can be cast with the /cast (spell) command.

use-as-respawn-location.description: Whether the mark should be used as a respawn location.

default-mark.description: Location of the default mark, format: world,x,y,z,yaw,pitch, but yaw and pitch are optional.

block-type.description: The type of block to materialize.

reset-delay.description: How long to wait before removing the materialized block. Set to 0 for permanent placement.

falling.description: Whether to make the materialized block fall (apply sand physics).

apply-physics.description: Whether to make the summoned block use normal physics. (Sand and gravel would fall, sugar cane would require sand/grass and water, etc.)

check-plugins.description: Whether the spell should check block-related plugins.

play-break-effect.description: Play the block break effect of the materialized block.

str-failed.description: Message sent to the caster when the spell fails.

patterns.description: Define a list of blocks to be used per row. If there are more rows than pattern lists, it will repeat. It will go through until the pattern lists are all used up.

restart-pattern-each-row.description: Explanation listed below the config option boxes, as it's too long to fit in here.

stretch-pattern.description: Generates 1 floor, then stretches that result until the height is reached.

area.description: Define an area of blocks to materialize.

height.description: Define the area's height to materialize.

fall-height.description: Change how high falling blocks fall from.

remove-blocks.description: Whether the blocks should be removed.

title.description: The title of the menu. Supports variable replacement.

delay.description: Delays the open event by server ticks. You should set this to at least 1 when chaining menu spells.

require-entity-target.description: If set to true targets entities to cast a spell on.

require-location-target.description: If set to true target locations to cast the spell at.

target-opens-menu-instead.description: If set to true, open the GUI for the targeted entity if require-entity-target is true.

bypass-normal-cast.description: In most cases, you don't want to change this. If set to false all sub-spells in all options will not be cast as a sub-spell, but as a main spell. By default, sub-spells are cast in partial mode, ignoring cooldowns and other things listed. But if you need cooldowns processed, ideally use the full mode (e.g. spell(mode=full)), because cast arguments are the preffered way of modifying sub-spell casting. This option set to false will ignore all cast arguments of all sub-spells, and force them to be cast in hard mode.

filler.description: An item to fill slots without items. Uses the Magic Item String (https://github.com/TheComputerGeek2/MagicSpells/wiki/Magic-Item-String) format.

stay-open-non-option.description: If set to true, when empty slots or fillers are clicked, the menu will stay open.

min-rows.description: The spell usually resizes the GUI to fit in all options. You can alternatively set a minimum amount of set rows.

options.description: A config section with keys that may be random, and values which follow Menu Option configuration.

duration.description: Buff spell duration specified in seconds before the buff expires. Set to 0 to define an infinite duration.

toggle.description: Defines whether the spell can be deactivated while still active. It will only apply cooldown when its duration fades.

targeted.description: Defines if this spell should become a targeted spell.

cancel-on-join.description: Defines whether the buff spell will cancel if the caster joins the game.

cancel-on-move.description: Defines whether the buff spell will cancel if the caster moves.

cancel-on-death.description: Defines whether the buff spell will cancel if the caster dies.

cancel-on-logout.description: Defines whether the buff spell will cancel if the caster leaves the game.

cancel-on-teleport.description: Defines whether the buff spell will cancel if the server emits a teleport event for the caster.

cancel-on-spell-cast.description: Defines whether the buff spell will cancel if the caster casts a spell.  this cancels all spells if the Spell Filter is left undefined.

cancel-on-take-damage.description: Defines whether the buff spell will cancel if the caster receives damage.

cancel-on-give-damage.description: Defines whether the buff spell will cancel if the caster deals damage to another entity.

cancel-on-change-world.description: Defines whether the buff spell will cancel if the caster switches worlds.

cancel-affects-target.description: If the buff spell is targeted, cancelling logic will be affected by and to the target instead.

real-time-duration.description: . When true, duration is tracked using real time, instead of ticks. As such, lag will not impact the duration of the buff spell.

power-affects-duration.description: Defines whether Spell Power can affect the spell's duration.

end-spell-from-target.description: Defines caster of the spell-on-end. If the spell isn't targeted, the "target" is technically the caster, so they cast the spell.

str-fade.description: Message sent after the buff ends.

spell-on-end.description: Sub-spell that will be cast whenever the buff spell reaches the end of its duration.

mob-chances.description: A list of the possible entity types and a chance to spawn them, in the format type chance:- type must be an Entity Type- chance must be an integer from 0 to 100

potion-effects.description: A list of potion effects to apply to the summoned minion. Follows the format: potionType duration strength ambient.- potionType should be a potion effect type- duration should be an integer for server ticks duration. It is optional with a default value of 600 ticks.- strength should be an integer. It is optional with a default value of 0.- ambient is for ambient particles and it is also optional. To enable it you write ambient at that parameter position, and omit it to disable it.

attributes.description: Attributes (https://github.com/TheComputerGeek2/MagicSpells/wiki/Attributes) to apply to the summoned minion.

main-hand.description: The Magic Item to be held in the minion's main hand.

off-hand.description: The Magic Item to be held in the minion's off-hand.

helmet.description: The Magic Item armor piece to show up in the helmet slot.

chestplate.description: The Magic Item armor piece to show up in the chestplate slot.

leggings.description: The Magic Item armor piece to show up in the leggings slot.

boots.description: The Magic Item armor piece to show up in the boots slot.

main-hand-drop-chance.description: The chance to drop the item in the minion's main hand.

off-hand-drop-chance.description: The chance to drop the item in the minion's off-hand.

helmet-drop-chance.description: The chance to drop the minion's helmet.

chestplate-drop-chance.description: The chance to drop the minion's chestplate.

leggings-drop-chance.description: The chance to drop the minion's leggings.

boots-drop-chance.description: The chance to drop the minion's boots.

spell-on-spawn.description: Sub-spell to cast when the minion spawns. This is achieved, code-wise, by having the caster cast a spell at the minion's location. The minion itself is not the one casting the spell. Must be able to target location or the mob itself.

spell-on-attack.description: Sub-spell to cast when the minion lands a hit. This is achieved, code-wise, by having the caster cast a spell at the minion's target. The minion itself is not the one casting the spell.

spell-on-death.description: Sub-spell to cast when the minion dies. This is achieved, code-wise, by having the caster cast a spell at the minion's location. The minion itself is not the one casting the spell. Must be able to target location.

spawn-offset.description: The offset to use when spawning a minion.

follow-range.description: The distance the minion should try to be from the caster.

follow-speed.description: The speed at which the minion will run to the caster when it reaches max-distance.

max-distance.description: The maximum distance the minion can be from the caster before running to them.

power-affects-health.description: Whether to let spell power influence the health option.

power-health-factor.description: The factor of which to change the mob's health based on spell power.

max-health.description: The maximum health the minion is able to have.

health.description: The beginning health of the mob on spawning.

minion-name.description: The name of the minion. If defined, the nameplate will always display above the mob's head.

gravity.description: Whether the minion should follow gravity.

baby.description: Not compatible with minion option - use the baby option under minion in that case.

prevent-sun-burn.description: Whether to prevent the minions from taking burn damage in the sun.

remove-vanilla.description: While you could use remove with the appropriate namespaced key (minecraft:...), the key sometimes differs from the VanillaGoal field name. This option also throws errors on invalid VanillaGoals listed - goals are often removed or renamed between minecraft versions.

remove.description: magicspells is the default namespace if left unspecified.

add.description: List of configuration sections to configure a custom goal to be added. Each should have a goal to specify the custom goal to add, an optional priority option (Integer, 0 by default), and a data configuration section to pass possibly required additional data to a goal.

seconds.description: Amount of seconds to remove from cooldowns. This applies before the multiplier is used.

multiplier.description: A number to multiply the cooldown by. Accepts decimal inputs. Applied after the seconds have been removed from the cooldown.

duration.description: Duration of the spell (in ticks).

reverse.description: Defines if the target should ride you instead.

cast-random-spell-instead.description: If no enable-x option is used, this selects a random spell to cast.

enable-custom-spell-cast-chance.description: Requires cast-random-spell-instead. If this is true, a random spell from the list is picked based on each spell's weights (spell:). For example, if two spells are listed, one with weight 3 and one with weight 1, the first will have 3 times as much chance to cast compared to the second spell.

enable-individual-chances.description: Requires cast-random-spell-instead. If this is true, each spell's chance to cast is defined by the chance listed after the spell name (spell:) in interval [0, 100]. NOTE: This can already be achieved with the chance subspell cast argument.

spells.description: A list of actions: either a sub-spell, DELAY , or a ranged variant DELAY  . If cast-random-spell-instead is set to true, spells must only contain spells, and if one of the enable-x options is set to true, a spell's chance/weight can be specified with spell:.

relative-offset.description: Relative offset applied at the start location.

spell-on-end.description: Spell to cast when all block waves disappear. (requires remove-previous-blocks set to false)

spell.description: Sub-spell to cast at each block, when a new wave forms.

spell-on-wave-remove.description: Sub-spell to cast when the previous wave is removed. (requires remove-previous-blocks set to true)

expand-interval.description: Interval between forming a new wave.

expanding-radius-change.description: Defines how big should each wave be, in blocks.

fake-blocks.description: . If set to false, real blocks will be placed instead of "packet" blocks that disappear when right-clicked.

point-blank.description: If set to true, the start location will be set to the caster's current location.

circle-shape.description: If set to true, nova will form a circle, if set to false, a square.

remove-previous-blocks.description: Whether it should remove the previous wave of blocks.

item.description: The item is placed in the slot and used to display the cooldown. Can be Magic Item String (https://github.com/TheComputerGeek2/MagicSpells/wiki/Magic-Item-String) or Magic Item Config (https://github.com/TheComputerGeek2/MagicSpells/wiki/Magic-Item-Config).

spell.description: The spell to display the cooldown of.

relative-offset.description: The offset to use relative to the player.

absolute-offset.description: A hard offset related to the player's coordinates, not where they're looking.

spell.description: Sub-spell to cast at an offset.

max-duration.description: Max duration, in seconds, of the orbit.

tick-interval.description: Defines how often, in ticks, to play the particle effect and check hitboxes.

vert-expand-delay.description: Defines how often, in ticks, to expand the orbit vertically. (requires vert-expand-radius)

horiz-expand-delay.description: Defines how often to expand the orbit horizontally. (requires horiz-expand-radius)

hit-radius.description: Defines how far from the center of the orbit to look for entities to hit.

vertical-hit-radius.description: Defines how far vertically from the center of the orbit to look for entities to hit.

orbit-radius.description: Defines how far from the center of the start location should the orbit be?

start-horiz-offset.description: Offset in degrees for the start yaw of the orbit.

start-pitch-offset.description: . Offset in degrees for the start pitch of the orbit.

start-angle-offset.description: . Offset in degrees for the starting angle of the orbit. Positive values follow in the direction of the orbit.

lock-start-yaw.description: . If true, the starting yaw is locked to 0 before offsets. When false, the start yaw is derived from the starting location.

lock-start-pitch.description: . If true, the starting pitch is locked to 0 before offsets. When false, the start pitch is derived from the starting location.

follow-pitch.description: . If true, the orbit's pitch is changed to following pitch changes from the targeted entity.

follow-yaw.description: . If true, the orbit is adjusted as the target changes yaw.

immune-ticks.description: . If immune-ticks >= 0, entities hit by the orbit are immune from collisions for the amount of ticks specified by the option.

constant-immune-ticks.description: . If false, immune-ticks is resolved per hit entity, instead of once at the start of the orbit.

vert-expand-radius.description: Defines how much to expand the orbit radius vertically. (requires vert-expand-delay)

horiz-expand-radius.description: Defines how much to expand the orbit radius horizontally. (requires horiz-expand-delay)

seconds-per-revolution.description: Defines how many seconds it takes the orbit to make a full circle. this may be 0 to create stationary orbits. It is also now rounded to the nearest tick-interval / 20 ticks. For example, with tick-interval: 1, 0.06 is rounded to 0.05; with tick-interval: 10, 5.7 is rounded to 5.5.

stop-on-hit-entity.description: Defines if the orbit should disappear when it hits an entity.

stop-on-hit-ground.description: Defines if the orbit should disappear when it hits the ground.

counter-clockwise.description: Defines if the orbit should rotate in a reverse way.

require-entity-target.description: Defines if the orbit should rotate around an entity target.

interactions.description: . Interactions define what happens when this orbit collides with another.

spell.description: Sub-spell to cast at the orbit location.

spell-on-hit-ground.description: Sub-spell to cast at the orbit location when it hits the ground.

spell-on-hit-entity.description: Sub-spell to cast at the entity target.

spell-damage-type.description: This is a custom damage type which can be used together with the ResistSpell. It's very optional.

damage-type.description: Defines the damage cause of this spell.  this option has been deprecated.

damage.description: Defines how much damage should be dealt to.

ignore-armor.description: Whether or not the spell should ignore armor that the target is wearing, ignores resistance.

check-plugins.description: Alert all plugins that this damage happened, allowing them to modify the event.

avoid-damage-modification.description: Defines if other plugins may modify the damage amount.

operation.description: Valid operations: translate (normal), difference, append, and regex (regexp)

parse-to-variable.description: Variable to store the result in after the operation is complete.

custom-name.description: Name that displays above the cloud.

particle-nameBetween 4.0 -16 you had to.description: Particle used to make the cloud.

wait-time-ticks.description: Defines the wait time before the cloud is applied. During this time, the particles only spawn at the center of the cloud.

duration-ticks.description: Defines the maximum cloud age after wait-time-ticks.

duration-ticks-on-use.description: Defines the number of ticks the duration of the cloud is reduced when the potion effects are applied.

reapplication-delay-ticks.description: Defines the number of ticks before applying the potion effects.

radius.description: Defines the radius of the cloud.

radius-on-use.description: Defines the amount the radius changes upon applying the effect. Normally negative.

radius-per-tick.description: Defines the amount the radius changes per tick. Normally negative.

use-gravity.description: Defines whether the cloud should respect gravity.

can-target-entities.description: Defines whether this spell, when cast directly, should only spawn on a targeted entity. This is checked before can-target-location.

can-target-location.description: Defines whether this spell, when cast directly, should only spawn on a targeted location. This is checked after can-target-entities˙.

Before 4.0 :potion-effects.description: String list of potion effects, each following the format: potion duration amplifier ambient particles icon.- potion: potion type- duration: integer defining server tick duration- amplifier: integer- ambient: boolean- particles: boolean- icon: boolean

can-cast-with-item.description: Defines if this spell can be cast with items.  with this false, spells can still be bound as cast items and cycled.

can-cast-by-command.description: Defines if this spell can be cast with the /cast (spell) command.

relative-offset.description: Relative offset applied at the start location. (Overrides all options above)

effect-offset.description: Effect offset applied at the projectile location.

projectile-acceleration.description: Increases the projectile's speed over time.

projectile-acceleration-delay.description: How often should it increase the projectile's speed? (in ticks).

projectile-turn.description: Rotates the projectile around this angle each tick it flies (forms a circle)

projectile-velocity.description: How fast the projectile moves, roughly in blocks per second.

projectile-vert-offset.description: See vertical-rotation instead due to this option's inaccurate rotations. This is an offset of the direction the projectile moves vertically, defined in tangent values.

projectile-horiz-offset.description: See horizontal-rotation instead due to this option's inaccurate rotations. This is an offset of the direction the projectile moves horizontally, defined in degrees.

vertical-rotation.description: Accurate rotation around the Z axis, defined in degrees.

horizontal-rotation.description: Accurate rotation around the Y axis, defined in degrees.

x-rotation.description: Accurate rotation around the X axis, defined in degrees.

projectile-gravity.description: How fast the projectile falls, roughly in blocks per second.

projectile-vert-gravity.description: How fast the projectile falls, roughly in blocks per second. (overrides projectile-gravity)

projectile-horiz-gravity.description: Applies gravity affecting its horizontal rotation (forms a spiral)

projectile-spread.description: Effectively the opposite of accuracy. A higher number means less accuracy.

projectile-vertical-spread.description: Effectively the opposite of vertical accuracy. A higher number means less accuracy. (overrides projectile-spread)

projectile-horizontal-spread.description: Effectively the opposite of horizontal accuracy. A higher number means less accuracy. (overrides projectile-spread)

tick-interval.description: Defines how often in ticks the projectile moves to check modifiers, hitboxes, play effects, etc.

spell-interval.description: Used with the hit-air-during option, casts the spell-on-tick on each spell-interval. Note that one interval of this runs every tick-interval * spell-interval ticks.

intermediate-effects.description: How often to play the particle effects between travelled locations

special-effect-interval.description: How often to play the particle effects. Note that one interval of this runs every tick-interval * special-effect-interval ticks.

max-distance.description: The maximum distance this particle is allowed to travel away from the start location.

max-duration.description: The maximum duration this projectile is allowed to exist.

tick-spell-limit.description: How many times can the spell-on-tick be casted? Works only if the value is more than 0

intermediate-hitboxes.description: How often to check hitboxes between travelled locations.  this also checks ground collisions.

max-entities-hit.description: How many targets can it hit before it disappears?

hit-radius.description: How far from the center of the projectile to look for entities to hit.

vertical-hit-radius.description: How far vertically from the center of the particle to look for entities to hit.

ground-hit-radius.description: How far from the center of the particle to look for ground blocks.

ground-vertical-hit-radius.description: How far vertically from the center of the particle to look for ground blocks.

ground-materials.description: A list of block materials, defined ground blocks for the projectile.

disallowed-ground-materials.description: The ground blocks the projectile should ignore. You can use this to override some blocks in the default path-able list.

hug-surface.description: Whether this projectile should hug the surface rather than follow an arc. If this is true, the projectile-gravity will be ignored.

height-from-surface.description: If hug-surface is true, this controls how high off the ground the projectile is.

max-height-check.description: . Checks how far above the projectile can be when hug-surface is set to true. This option does the check as the projectile travels.

start-height-check.description: . Checks how far above the projectile can be when hug-surface is set to true. This option does the check only when you cast the projectile.

controllable.description: If set to true, the projectile will follow your cursor's direction

check-plugins.description: Alerts plugins of this projectile's movement so that they can tap into MagicSpells and control its movements.

change-pitch.description: If set to false, the projectile's start location will set its pitch to 0

hit-ground.description: Whether the projectile should trigger the spell upon running into the ground.

hit-air-at-end.description: Whether the projectile should trigger the spell if it reaches max-distance without hitting anything.

hit-air-during.description: Whether the projectile should trigger the spell all the way it travels.

hit-air-after-duration.description: Whether the projectile should trigger the spell if it reaches max-duration without hitting anything.

stop-on-hit-ground.description: Whether the projectile should stop upon hitting the ground. If this is false, it will pass through the ground and will not stop unless max-distance or max-duration is set

stop-on-hit-entity.description: Whether the projectile should stop upon hitting an entity. If this is false, it will continue through and could hit multiple targets.

stop-on-modifier-fail.description: Whether the projectile should disappear if projectile-modifiers will fail

allow-caster-interact.description: Whether the caster can create interactions without other players.

ignore-passable-blocks.description: . Sets if the projectile ignores passable blocks.

power-affects-velocity.description: Whether the spell power affects the velocity of the projectile.

fluid-collision-mode.description: . Sets how the projectile interacts with fluids.

hit-self.description: Whether the projectile should hit the caster.

hit-players.description: Whether the projectile should hit players.

hit-non-players.description: Whether the projectile should hit non-players.

projectile-modifiers.description: Modifiers (https://github.com/TheComputerGeek2/MagicSpells/wiki/Modifiers) for the projectile, if the modifier check won't pass, the projectile will stop flying.

interactions.description: Interactions define what happens when this projectile collides with another.

can-target.description: A general spell option that overrides the hit-... options.

spell.description: The default sub-spell to cast on hit. It must be a targeted spell that can hit the ground or an entity, depending on how the options above are set up.

spell-on-hit-air.description: Sub-spell to cast when it reaches max distance. It must be a targeted spell that can hit the ground or an entity, depending on how the options above are set up. (requires hit-air-at-end)

spell-on-hit-self.description: Sub-spell to cast when the projectile hits the caster. It must be a targeted spell that can hit the ground or an entity, depending on how the options above are set up. (requires hit-self)

spell-on-tick.description: Sub-spell to cast when the projectile flies. It must be a targeted spell that can hit the ground, depending on how the options above are set up. (requires hit-air-during and spell-interval)

spell-on-hit-ground.description: Sub-spell to cast when the projectile hits the ground. It must be a targeted spell that can hit the ground, depending on how the options above are set up. (requires hit-ground)

spell-on-hit-entity.description: Sub-spell to cast when the projectile hits a target. It must be a targeted spell that can hit the ground or an entity, depending on how the options above are set up. (requires hit-players or hit-non-players or can-target list)

spell-on-duration-end.description: Sub-spell to cast when the projectile reaches its max-duration. It must be a targeted spell that can hit the ground, depending on how the options above are set up. (requires hit-air-after-duration)

spell-on-modifier-fail.description: Sub-spell to cast when the projectile's modifier list fails. It must be a targeted spell that can hit the ground, depending on how the options above are set up. (requires projectile-modifiers)

spell-on-entity-location.description: Sub-spell to cast when the projectile hits a target. It must be a targeted spell that can hit the ground.

chance.description: The likelihood that the passive spell will cast its sub-spells, in the interval [0, 100].

delay.description: The amount of time in ticks the passive spell will wait before casting spells.

ignore-cancelled.description: Even if the trigger was cancelled, for instance through other plugins, the passive spell's sub-spells will still be cast when enabled.

send-failure-messages.description: If the casting failed due to the passive's cooldown or reagents (cost) missing, this controls whether the str-on-cooldown, str-missing-reagents & str-cost should be sent.

cancel-default-action.description: Cancel the event that activated this passive spell. For example, cancelling a takedamage trigger would negate the damage. Note that only triggers with the Cancellable property can be cancelled.

require-cancelled-event.description: If enabled the trigger must be cancelled for the passive spell to cast its sub-spells.

remove-paste.description: Should the schematic be removed on server stop/reload?

can-cast-by-command.description: Defines if this spell can be cast with the /cast (spell) command.

phasable-blocks.description: A list of block types that can be phased through. If this option is empty or left out, all blocks can be phased through.

non-phasable-blocks.description: A list of block types that cannot be phased through.

max-distance.description: The maximum distance the caster can travel through solid matter to reach open air. This includes the distance leading up to the wall (the range).

use-target-variables.description: If true the spell uses the target's variables. If false it uses the caster’s variables.

set-target-placeholders.description: Target of placeholders. If true it parses using the target’s placeholders. If false it uses the caster's.

skull-name-radius.description: Skull name displayed if the player is outside of the radius if radius is used.

end-spell-from-target.description: Defines caster of the spell-on-end. If the spell isn't targeted, the "target" is technically the caster, so they cast the spell.

str-fade.description: Message sent after the buff ends.

spell-on-end.description: Sub-spell that will be cast whenever the buff spell reaches the end of its duration.

update-interval.description: The interval between the bar update

symbol.description: Symbol used to create the bar

yaw.description: Modifies yaw of the location by the set value.

pitch.description: Modifies pitch of the location by the set value.

relative-offset.description: Relative offset applied at the target's location.

str-no-landing-spot.description: Message sent to the caster when there isn't a safe spot to teleport.

vanilla-sheep-shearing.description: . If this is false, the options below define the explicit wool drop amounts and their color.

min-wool-drop.description: Minimal amount of wool to drop.

max-wool-drop.description: Maximum amount of wool to drop.

random-wool-color.description: Should the wool's color be random.

prevent-cast.description: Whether to stop the target from casting spells.

prevent-chat.description: Whether to stop the target from using chat.

prevent-commands.description: Whether to stop the target from using commands.

notify-helper-spells.description: . When true, casting such spells that pass the spell filter will display the str-silenced message to the caster.

notify-passive-spells.description: . When true, casting such spells that pass the spell filter will display the str-silenced message to the caster.

spell-on-denied-cast.description: Sub-spell to cast whenever the target's spell was silenced.

spell-on-denied-chat.description: Sub-spell to cast whenever the target's chat was silenced.

spell-on-denied-command.description: Sub-spell to cast whenever the target's command was silenced.

duration.description: How long, in ticks, the silence spell lasts.

str-silenced.description: String message sent to any player who is silenced.

size.description: Modifies the Slime's size through Assignment as if it was a numeric variable.

ignore-slot-bounds.description: This spell will send errors in the console if the bounds of slots are not respected. This property can be set to true for the error to be ignored.

slot.description: This property can be a pure integer of the slot, or it can be a string - a variable name. If it's a variable, the spell will set the selected slot to the target's value of the variable.

entity.description: Note before 4.0 , equivalent spell options or their defaults that configure the entity override what was set in Entity Data (e.g. whatever is set for gravity is overridden by the spell's gravity, either what you set it to or false by default).

duration.description: Duration of the mob (in ticks).

fire-ticks.description: Amount of fire ticks applied to the mob.

location.description: Location where the mob should be spawned. May be: casteroffset:, offset:, caster, target, focus (for a target location spell, the location will be the target), or random (will spawn the mob randomly in a radius determined by spell power).

gravity.description: Defines if the mob should obey gravity.

remove-ai.description: Defines if the mob's ai be removed on spawn.

invulnerable.description: . When true, makes the entity invulnerable to all kinds of damage.

remove-mob.description: Removes the mob after MS reload.

center-location.description: . Centers the spawn location for the entity.

add-look-at-player-ai.description: Adds mob goal where the mob constantly looks at players. Requires remove-ai.

cancel-attack.description: . Determines whether or not the attacks done by a spawned entity are canceled when attack-spell is specified.

attack-spell.description: Sub-spell which is casted when the mob attacks.

spell-on-spawn.description: Sub-spell which is cast on spawn with the entity or its location as the target if applicable.

spell-on-death.description: . Sub-spell cast on the spawned entity when it dies.

spell-on-target.description: . Sub-spell on the target of the spawned entity when it changes targets.

potion-effects.description: Potion effect list for the mob. Each follows format potion duration strength ambient.- potion: potion type- duration: integer duration in server ticks (600 if unspecified)- strength: integer (0 if unspecified)- ambient: write ambient for ambient particles (false if unspecified)

cancel-gravity.description: Defines if the tnt should cancel gravity.

cancel-explosion.description: Defines if the tnt should cancel its explosion.

prevent-block-damage.description: Defines if block damage should be prevented.

spell.description: Sub-spell to cast at the TNT's explode location.

can-cast-with-item.description: Defines if this spell can be cast with items.  with this false, spells can still be bound as cast items and cycled.

can-cast-by-command.description: Defines if this spell can be cast with the /cast (spell) command.

spell-class.description: Aside from these general options, every class has its options - you can find them along with their class name in the Spell List (https://github.com/TheComputerGeek2/MagicSpells/wiki/Spell-List). The spell class is the Java class' fully qualified class name - if the class is from MS, then com.nisovin.spells may be omitted.

name.description: String that gets displayed as a spell name when scrolling through a spell list. We call it the external spell name because when listing a spell with this option as a sub-spell, you wouldn't use this name, but the internal spell name. It supports color codes, but when trying to cast the spell using the cast command, you have to specify the spell in quotations, without color codes.

always-granted.description: If enabled, all players will be taught this spell. For more about permissions check this page of our wiki.

permission-name.description: It defaults to the internal spell name, but it can be changed to any string. Multiple spells can have the same permission-name. For more about permissions visit this page.

incantations.description: A list of strings that would cast the spell when sent in chat by a player. They may even be prepended with / to act as fake commands. You can additionally register them as a command alias in Bukkit's commands.yml so they don't appear red in chat preview. There's a special syntax where if you list an incantation as one_word * then in chat type one_word arg1 arg2 arg..., the spell with that incantation will be cast with those spell cast args strings, which can be referenced by the %arg placeholder in options that support expressions.

description.description: A string that would be sent to chat when using the Help spell.

broadcast-range.description: Distance that spells messages (such as str-cast-others) are broadcast from the casting player to other players nearby.

cost.description: A string list of reagents required for the spell to be cast.

str-cost.description: String that gets displayed under Cost on the Help spell.

str-missing-reagents.description: String sent to the caster if the caster does not have the reagents required for the spell to cast successfully.

modifiers.description: View further documentation on this page.

target-modifiers.description: Conditions for the target to consider for the target handler. View further documentation on this page.

str-modifier-failed.description: String sent to the caster when but a modifier that was specified failed.

location-modifiers.description: Conditions for the target location to consider for the target handler. View further documentation on this page.

variable-mods-cast.description: Variables are modified for the caster whether the spell failed to cast or not. Learn how to modify variables here.

variable-mods-casted.description: Variables are modified only if the spell is cast successfully. Learn how to modify variables here.

variable-mods-target.description: Can be used in targeted spells to change variable values of targets. Learn how to modify variables here.

tags.description: This is a list of strings which some spells support to refer to a group of spells that are tagged with a specific tag. This is most commonly used in a Spell Filter (https://github.com/TheComputerGeek2/MagicSpells/wiki/Spell-Filter) which some spells like the SilenceSpell or No Magic Zones (https://github.com/TheComputerGeek2/MagicSpells/wiki/No-Magic-Zones) use.

delay.description: Defines delay in ticks before the effect is played.

chance.description: Defines the chance of the effect playing, ranging from (0, 1).

z-offset.description: Defines offset of the effect location on the Z axis.

height-offset.description: Same as above, but for the Y axis.

forward-offset.description: Same as above, but for the X-axis.

offset.description: Vector offset instead of the above properties.

relative-offset.description: Same as above, but it's relative.

modifiers.description: Modifiers (https://github.com/TheComputerGeek2/MagicSpells/wiki/Modifiers) which determine whether the spell effect should be played or not.

caster-modifiers.description: Same as above, but it exclusively checks against the caster of the spell.

target-modifiers.description: Same as above, but it exclusively checks against the target of the spell.

location-modifiers.description: Same as above, but it exclusively checks against the targeted location of the spell.

spells.description: List of spells the filter will include, ignoring other spells.

denied-spells.description: List of spells not included in the filter.

spell-tags.description: List of spell tags the filter will include, ignoring other tags.

denied-spell-tags.description: List of spell tags not included.

allowed-spells.description: Spells allowed to be bound by this Bind spell.

default-uses.description: If no uses are passed with spell cast arguments, this is the amount used.

palette.description: The palette index of the structure to use, starting at 0, or -1 to pick a random palette.

integrity.description: Determines how damaged the building should look by randomly skipping blocks to place. This value can range from 0 to 1. With 0 removing all blocks and 1 spawning the structure in pristine condition.

stun-monitor.description: Defines if the stun spell should stun the target's monitor movement.

stun-body.description: Defines if the stun spell should stun the target's body movement.

use-target-location.description: Defines whether the target should be teleported to the location they were at when they were targeted (true) or if the location they moved from (false).

require-exact-name.description: Defines if the spell should require the exact player name.

require-acceptance.description: Defines if the spell requires the player's acceptance.

accept-command.description: Accept command - what they write after a /.

require-lesser-health-percent.description: Defines whether to require that the caster have a lower health percentage than the target.

switch-back.description: Defines should caster swap back with target after some time. Defines in ticks.

tag.description: Defines the tag name. Supports variable replacement (of caster's variables).

operation.description: Valid operations: add (or insert), remove (or take), and clear.

block.description: Block material the platform is made of.

duration.description: Duration of the platform (int ticks).

touch-check-interval.description: Defines how often in server ticks it should check players touching the carpet.

circle.description: Defines whether the carpets should form a circle.

remove-on-touch.description: Defines whether the carpets should disappear when someone touches it.

spell-on-touch.description: Sub-spell to cast when someone touches the carpet.

spells.description: A list of sub-spells to cast, in order. You can delay spells individually using sub-spell cast modes, or alternatively you can also list DELAY  to delay all subsequent spells by the specified delay.

y-offset.description: Modifies the height of the targeted location

point-blank.description: Whether the spell should be centered on the player, or if it should be targeted. If this is true, the spell is centered on the casting player. If it's false, it will be centered at the targeted block.

pass-targeting.description: When false, sub-spells will recheck their individual can-target lists.

stop-on-fail.description: When true, the spell will stop executing spells if one of them fails.

stop-on-success.description: When true, stops sub-spell processing when a sub-spell successfully casts.

require-entity-target.description: Whether this spell requires the caster to target an entity.

rotation-offset.description: Which direction to throw the block, in degrees. A value of 0 is forward, 180 is backward, etc.

apply-spell-power-to-velocity.description: Whether to apply the Spell Power for this spell to the velocity.

drop-item.description: Whether to drop an item if the block is unable to land properly.

remove-blocks.description: If this is true, the thrown blocks will be removed shortly after they have landed.

call-target-event.description: Whether being hit by a falling block should count as being targeted by this spell.

ensure-spell-cast.description: If this is true, the plugin will constantly monitor the thrown block to make sure the spell goes off. This may harm performance, so if you don't need this feature you can turn it off.

sticky-blocks.description: If this is true, it will cause blocks to “stick” to walls, rather than hit them and fall.

toggle.description: Defines whether the spell can be deactivated while still active. It will only apply cooldown when its duration fades.

targeted.description: Defines if this spell should become a targeted spell.

cancel-on-join.description: Defines whether the buff spell will cancel if the caster joins the game.

cancel-on-move.description: Defines whether the buff spell will cancel if the caster moves.

cancel-on-death.description: Defines whether the buff spell will cancel if the caster dies.

cancel-on-logout.description: Defines whether the buff spell will cancel if the caster leaves the game.

cancel-on-teleport.description: Defines whether the buff spell will cancel if the server emits a teleport event for the caster.

cancel-on-spell-cast.description: Defines whether the buff spell will cancel if the caster casts a spell.  this cancels all spells if the Spell Filter is left undefined.

cancel-on-take-damage.description: Defines whether the buff spell will cancel if the caster receives damage.

cancel-on-give-damage.description: Defines whether the buff spell will cancel if the caster deals damage to another entity.

cancel-on-change-world.description: Defines whether the buff spell will cancel if the caster switches worlds.

cancel-affects-target.description: If the buff spell is targeted, cancelling logic will be affected by and to the target instead.

real-time-duration.description: . When true, duration is tracked using real time, instead of ticks. As such, lag will not impact the duration of the buff spell.

power-affects-duration.description: Defines whether Spell Power can affect the spell's duration.

end-spell-from-target.description: Defines caster of the spell-on-end. If the spell isn't targeted, the "target" is technically the caster, so they cast the spell.

str-fade.description: Message sent after the buff ends.

spell-on-end.description: Sub-spell that will be cast whenever the buff spell reaches the end of its duration.

stair-type.default: platform-type unless it was set to cobblestone or oak planks, in which case it uses according stairs for the platform.

can-cast-with-item.description: Defines if this spell can be cast with items.  with this false, spells can still be bound as cast items and cycled.

can-cast-by-command.description: Defines if this spell can be cast with the /cast (spell) command.

wall-types.description: List of block types the wall is made of.

str-no-target.description: Message sent to the caster when it's unable to create a wall.

str-at-cap.default: "You have too many effects at once."
str-at-cap.description: . Message sent to the caster when they have reached cap-per-entity.

spell-on-break.description: Sub-spell which is casted at the broken wall block.

wall-duration.description: how many ticks the wall is activated for.

cap-per-entity.description: . If too many walls are placed, the spell fails with the message from str-at-cap.

check-plugins.description: Whether it should check world-related plugins.

prevent-drops.description: Whether it should prevent block drops from the wall.

always-on-ground.description: Whether the wall should always be spawned at the ground.

prevent-breaking.description: Whether the wall is unable to break.

check-plugins-per-block.description: Whether it should check world-related plugins.

always-fly.description: . When true, the target of the spell cannot land.

enable-max-y.description: . This fixes a bug where you could not set max-y to be below Y level 0.

launch-speed.description: If this is greater than zero, the caster will be thrown into the air upon casting the spell (and won't fall because they'll be flying). The bigger the number, the higher they'll go.

max-y.description: The maximum height y-value a player can fly.

max-altitude.description: The maximum altitude (blocks above ground) the player can fly.

cancel-on-land.description: Whether the wind walk effect is cancelled upon landing on the ground.

allowed-block-types.description: A list of allowed block types to break.

disallowed-block-types.description: A list of block types not allowed to be broken.

str-cant-zap.description: Message to send when the caster is unable to zap the targeted block.

drop-block.description: Defines whether to drop the block or not.

drop-normal.description: Defines whether to drop the normal type of that block.

check-plugins.description: Alert other plugins of this change.

play-break-effect.description: Whether to play the block break effect when cast.
